title: Magnet vs Iron Reasoning Tool
description: An interactive drag-and-test tool for Grade 6 Science – Chapter 4: Exploring Magnets. Students drag three identical metal bars (X, Y, Z) into test zones to observe attraction/repulsion, then classify each bar as magnet or iron.

additionalProps.instructionText: Drag each bar near the others to test for attraction or repulsion. Test all three pairs. Then decide which two are magnets and which is iron.
additionalProps.correctExplanation: Only magnets can repel each other. Since X and Y showed repulsion, they must both be magnets. Z only attracted every bar — so Z is iron!

instructions_for_student: Drag each bar near the others to test for attraction or repulsion. Test all three pairs. Then decide which two are magnets and which is iron.
subject: Science
activity_reference: Question 5 – Reshma's Three Metal Bars (Textbook p.75)

instructionText.description: Instruction shown to the student at the top of the tool
instructionText.default: Drag each bar near the others to test for attraction or repulsion. Test all three pairs. Then decide which two are magnets and which is iron.

magnetPair.description: The two bar IDs that are magnets. These will repel each other and attract iron.

ironBar.description: The bar ID that is iron (non-magnet). It only attracts and never repels.

correctExplanation.description: Explanation shown when student correctly identifies the magnets and iron bar

themeColor.description: Accent color (hex). Affects active states, badges, and highlights.

darkMode.description: Enable dark theme for projector/classroom use

_interaction_logic.description: How the tool determines repulsion vs attraction for each pair

rules.rationale: Two magnets can repel when like poles face each other

rules.condition: One bar is in magnetPair, the other is ironBar
rules.rationale: A magnet always attracts iron regardless of orientation

rules.condition: Both bars are ironBar (impossible with 3 bars)

student_discovery_path: Test X-Y → sees REPULSION (red arrows) → knows at least one combination of X,Y shows repulsion
Test X-Z → sees ATTRACTION (green arrows) → Z attracted, not iron identification yet
Test Y-Z → sees ATTRACTION (green arrows) → Z attracted again
Conclusion: X and Y repelled each other → both magnets. Z never repelled → iron.

_agent_examples.description: Default NCERT Q5 — X and Y are magnets, Z is iron

call.title: Identify the Magnets — Reshma's Bar Test

_agent_examples.description: Variant — X and Z are magnets, Y is iron

additionalProps.correctExplanation: X and Z repelled each other — they are both magnets. Y only attracted, never repelled — so Y is iron.

_agent_examples.description: Dark mode classroom variant — Y and Z are magnets, X is iron

additionalProps.correctExplanation: Y and Z showed repulsion — both are magnets. X only attracted both bars — it is iron.

use_this_tool_when: Student asks about Reshma's three-bar identification question (NCERT Ch4 Q5)
Question involves distinguishing magnets from iron using repulsion test
Student needs to understand why repulsion uniquely identifies magnets
Drag-and-test interactive experiment about magnet properties
do_not_use_when: Question is about U-clip counts on bar magnet positions (use magnet_selection_tool)
Question is about compass direction or north-south alignment
Open-ended short answer question (respond with text)
